# Scope Statement  

## Purpose of This Document

This Project Scope Statement formally defines the **project scope baseline at a
narrative level**, specifying:

- What is included in the project
- What is explicitly excluded
- The boundaries of the MVP
- Key deliverables and acceptance criteria
- Constraints and assumptions that shape execution

This document is a **mandatory PMBOK artifact** and, together with the **WBS and
WBS Dictionary**, forms the **Scope Baseline** of the project.

---

## Project Scope Description

The project will design, develop, and validate an **AI-enabled environmental
intelligence pipeline** to support **automated screening of AUV imagery**, with
the primary objective of **protecting and scaling ecological analysis of benthic
environments**.

The solution will apply **Computer Vision techniques** to classify still images
as:

- **Benthic-relevant** (ecologically significant)
- **Non-benthic / non-ecologically relevant**

Automation is introduced strictly as an **enabler**, with **human-in-the-loop
validation** maintained as a mandatory control to preserve ecological
confidence.

---

## Product Scope (What Will Be Delivered)

### Core Product Capabilities (MVP)

- Automated screening of AUV still images using AI/CV
- Batch, offline-first processing of inspection datasets
- AI-assisted annotation workflows
- Precision-oriented binary classification
- Ecological validation and acceptance by environmental SMEs
- Versioned datasets and model artifacts
- Traceability and audit support

---

### Target Operational Context

- Initial validation in at least one field (Viola or Espadarte)
- Demonstrated generalization across two fields
- Use in environmental baseline studies and inspection support

---

## Project Deliverables

| Deliverable | Description | Acceptance Authority |
|--------------|---------------------|-----------------|
| AI Screening Model | Binary CV model for benthic relevance | Environmental SME Lead |
| Inference Pipeline | Offline batch screening capability | Data Science Lead |
| Annotation Guidelines | Ecological labeling rules | Environmental SMEs |
| Validation Reports | Performance and ecological assurance | Sponsor / NOAA |
| Versioned Datasets | Curated, auditable datasets | Data Governance |
| Project Documentation | Governance and technical artifacts | Project Sponsor |

---

## Acceptance Criteria (High-Level)

The project deliverables will be accepted when:

- Ecological Data Protection Index (KPI‑3) meets or exceeds agreed threshold 
  (≥ 85–90%)
- Manual screening effort is reduced by ≥ 30% without ecological degradation
- Environmental screening lead time is reduced by ≥ 25%
- Environmental SMEs validate usability and confidence
- Assets are versioned, auditable, and reusable

---

## Project Exclusions (Explicitly Out of Scope)

The following are **explicitly excluded** from this project scope:

- Real-time or near-real-time processing
- Full taxonomic classification of organisms
- Autonomous decision-making without human validation
- Advanced multimodal fusion (sonar, bathymetry) beyond preparatory design
- AUV operations, mission planning, or data acquisition
- External data sharing or publication workflows

---

## Constraints

- No pre-labeled dataset available at project start
- Natural class imbalance in subsea imagery
- Offline-first processing requirement
- Environmental variability (turbidity, lighting, backscatter)
- Use of license-compliant AI frameworks only
- Availability of environmental SMEs for validation

---

## Assumptions

- Still imagery is sufficient for MVP validation
- Environmental SMEs will be available for iterative validation
- Compute resources are available to support batch processing
- Binary classification provides sufficient early value
- MVP results will inform future expansion decisions

---

## Scope Control

- Any change to this Scope Statement requires a **formal Change Request**
- Changes must be evaluated for:
  - Ecological risk
  - Impact on OKRs and KPIs
  - Schedule and cost implications
- Scope changes are subject to Sponsor approval via Stage‑Gates

---

## Relationship to Other Scope Artifacts

This Project Scope Statement is complemented by:

- Work Breakdown Structure (WBS)
- WBS Dictionary
- Requirements Specification

Together, these documents define the **Scope Baseline**.
